FT_IC1.evidence: Paper analyzes data from the U.S. ABCD Study with 7,614 participants, Year 2 (2018-2020), ages 10-13

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis; reports neuroimaging findings from structural MRI with quantitative morphology measures

FT_IC2.evidence: Social media use (average daily hours per day) and social media addiction (SMAQ) as IVs paired with brain morphology (cortical thickness, volume, surface area) as DVs; neural outcome is individual-trait-level

FT_IC3.evidence: Reports standardized beta coefficients (β = -1.916, SE 0.6 for cortical volume), p-values, and FDR-corrected results across ROI and vertexwise analyses

FT_IC4.evidence: Quantitative results reported throughout: regression coefficients with standard errors and p-values for cortical morphology associations

FT_EC7_reason: Design is cross-sectional (all Year 2 data); FT-EC7 applies only to longitudinal designs with SM as DV-only
notes: Multiple analyses examining social media use with neural outcomes; both social media time and addiction scale examined

FT_IC1.evidence: Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development Study, N=8292, mean age 12.0 years at Year 2 (2018-2020)

FT_IC5.evidence: Original prospective longitudinal analysis with generalized structural equation modeling (GSEM) testing mediation pathways

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed article in American Journal on Addictions (2026)

FT_IC2.evidence: Social media time at Year 2 (hours per day, IV) paired with nicotine experimentation at Year 4 (DV); outcome is individual-trait-level (substance use behavior)

FT_IC3.evidence: Reports regression coefficients for direct effects (B=0.14, 95% CI 0.08-0.20, p<0.001) and indirect effects through mediator

FT_IC4.evidence: Quantitative results: regression coefficients, indirect effects, proportions mediated (5.97%, 95% CI 1.27%-10.67%, p=.013)

FT_EC7_reason: Design is longitudinal but SM is IV (not DV-only); EC7 requires SM to be DV in every analysis with no SM-as-IV anywhere
notes: Longitudinal mediation analysis; social media as predictor of nicotine experimentation two years later

FT_IC1.evidence: ABCD Study Year 5 (2021-2023) cross-sectional data, N=11,530 adolescents ages 13-16

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis; multivariable linear regression with cluster-robust standard errors

FT_IC6.evidence: Published peer-reviewed research note in BMC Research Notes (2026)

FT_IC2.evidence: Dating app use (online dating apps; platform-specific SM variable per criteria) as IV paired with depression symptoms (mental health/individual-trait-level) as DV

FT_IC3.evidence: Reports standardized beta coefficient (β 0.42), 95% confidence interval (0.08-0.76), p=0.017 for dating app use and depression association

FT_IC4.evidence: Quantitative results reported: standardized beta coefficients with 95% CIs and p-values for adjusted and unadjusted models

FT_EC7_reason: Design is cross-sectional; FT-EC7 applies only to longitudinal designs
notes: Cross-sectional association between dating app use and depression; sensitivity analyses confirm robust findings
